reasoning: Ramzan Kadyrov has a long history of making public requests to step down (2016, 2017, 2022) only to be "forced" to stay by Putin. This same pattern played out in early May 2025: Kadyrov requested to be relieved of his duties around May 5/6, but closely followed this by meeting Putin on May 7. Validated reports from May 8 (such as from ISW) indicate Putin refused the resignation. Kadyrov subsequently walked back his statements, emphasizing that the decision is ultimately Putin's and that he is a loyal soldier. With the June 1, 2025 resolution date less than three weeks away from the May 12 knowledge cutoff, the timeline is extremely tight for any sudden, real departure without a documented transition plan. Although his health has been the subject of persistent rumors and might eventually force a succession, an official exit within this 20-day timeframe following a high-profile rejection of his resignation by Putin is highly improbable. I am assigning a minimum probability of 0.05, constrained by forecasting best practices against absolute certainty, reflecting a very small tail risk of an abrupt ouster or sudden incapacitation/death.
